prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
1. *"Lo! In this third quarter of the Year of Our Lord Two Thousand and Twenty-Four, the sorcerers of ‘Salesforce’ didst summon forth a great host of ‘leads’ from the aether, yet many did flee like specters at dawn when the ‘churn rate’ didst rise by two-and-twenty percent—verily, the demons of ‘customer attrition’ work in mysterious ways."*

2. *"The alchemists of ‘Revenue Operations’ did brew a potent elixir called ‘ARR’ (which some whisper stands for *Auri Regis Redditum*, though none dare ask), and though the cauldron bubbled with +14% growth, the steward of coin did frown, for the ‘burn rate’ did devour gold as a dragon doth a maiden’s dowry."*

3. *"A great schism hath arisen betwixt the ‘Product’ and ‘Marketing’ guilds, for the former did craft a ‘feature’ most wondrous—‘AI-driven hyper-personalization’—yet the latter could not sell it, crying, ‘The peasants know not what ‘LLM’ meaneth!’ And thus did the ‘OKRs’ wither like crops in a cursed field."*

4. *"Most puzzling of all: the CEO, a man of strange and shifting humors, did proclaim from his ivory tower (or ‘Zoom call,’ as the scribes now name it) that ‘we’re doubling down on land-and-expand,’ yet when pressed, he spake only in riddles of ‘stickiness’ and ‘net dollar retention,’ leaving this humble chronicler to wonder if he hath been cursed to document the madness of merchants or the prophecies of a false oracle."*
